statements.impact_statement: CVE-2026-33634 affects trivy's credential-handling behavior when invoked from GitHub Actions pipelines. DockPit runs trivy exclusively as a local CLI inside its own server/agent container for on-demand image scanning; the CI-specific code path is never reached.

CVE-2025-15558 requires a malicious docker CLI plugin binary to be dropped into ~/.docker/cli-plugins or /usr/libexec/docker/cli-plugins. DockPit ships only the official docker-compose plugin; there is no mechanism for end users to install third-party plugins into the running container.

CVE-2026-34040 is an authorization bypass in the Moby engine daemon's authz handler. DockPit embeds github.com/docker/docker only via the docker-compose CLI for API type definitions; the Moby daemon binary and its authz handler are never compiled into our image or executed at runtime.

libnghttp2-14 is installed transitively as part of debian:stable-slim but is not linked by any binary DockPit executes. The Rust server uses rustls for TLS and does not depend on libnghttp2; the Go binaries (docker-compose, docker CLI, trivy) are statically linked and bundle their own HTTP/2 implementation.
